title: artocarpus nitidus subsp. humilis
intro: Artocarpus nitidus subsp. humilis, commonly known as the Fijian breadfruit, is a subspecies of breadfruit native to the Pacific Islands. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities, making it useful in treating various health issues. The health benefits of Artocarpus nitidus subsp. humilis include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve digestive health, and even help manage diabetes. Herbal preparations of the plant are often made into teas, tinctures, or infusions, which are consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
